title: Recurrent Discrete Hazard Model With Stop Resets
summary: This target is literally a one-step hazard.
rationale: This target is literally a one-step hazard. Discrete-time survival literature supports using classification models on person-period data, and recurrent-event formulations map well to repeated pit stops within a race. Earlier generic survival ideas likely underused the reset structure after each actual stop.
implementation_hint: Model hazard separately within each stint or with explicit reset counters: baseline by `TyreLife`, `Stint`, and `RaceProgress`; add interactions with compound, wet/dry regime, and prior stop count. Try cloglog or logit hazard heads and convert OOF hazard for the current row directly into a blend feature for the main model.
expected_effect: Should sharpen probability shape around stint-specific windows and reduce confusion between first-stop and later-stop behavior.
risk: If expanded improperly, person-period framing can duplicate rows and distort validation. Keep splits at race-year level and ensure every transformed row inherits its parent fold.
